title: Reading and Use of English - Part 6
description: You are going to read an article from which six sentences have been removed. Choose from the sentences A–G the one which fits each gap (37–42). There is one extra sentence which you do not need to use.

content.title: A Matter of Taste: Life as a Professional Food Taster
content.text: Most people would consider being paid to eat for a living the perfect career. Professional food tasters, however, tell a more complicated story. Far from being an endless pleasure, the job demands extraordinary sensory discipline, rigorous training and a willingness to remain objective about foods that may be deeply unpleasant. (37) [37] The professional taster's palate is a precision instrument, trained over years to detect minute differences in flavour, texture and aroma. [/37] || The food industry employs tasters in several distinct roles. Quality control tasters check that mass-produced foods conform consistently to established specifications. A slight change in the fat content of a biscuit or the acidity of a tomato sauce that a consumer would barely notice must be identified and reported. (38) [38] This requires extraordinary attention to detail and a highly trained sensory memory. [/38] || Product development tasters work at the creative end of the business, evaluating new recipes and flavour combinations. (39) [39] Their role requires not only accurate sensory perception, but also the ability to articulate complex taste experiences in language that non-specialists can understand. [/39] || The vocabulary of professional tasting is vast. Where an ordinary person might describe a wine as 'nice', a trained taster might identify specific notes of blackcurrant, cedar, graphite and dried herbs, along with assessments of acidity, tannin, body and finish. (40) [40] This shared technical language enables precise communication between tasters, food scientists and product managers. [/40] || Health and lifestyle considerations are an underappreciated part of the job. Tasters must avoid strong flavours — coffee, spicy food, cigarettes — before working sessions, and many report that the constant sampling, even in small quantities, affects their appetite for normal meals. (41) [41] Some tasters develop what is known as 'palate fatigue', a temporary dulling of sensory acuity that can compromise evaluations if rest periods are not observed. [/41] || Despite these challenges, professional food tasters describe a deep satisfaction in work that is simultaneously scientific and sensory. (42) [42] The knowledge that a product millions of people enjoy has been made better by their judgement is, for most, reward enough. [/42]

paragraphs.A: Developing this vocabulary is one of the most time-consuming aspects of professional taster training.
paragraphs.B: The ability to communicate those findings clearly and consistently is equally important to the technical skill.
paragraphs.C: They are often the first people outside the laboratory to encounter a product that may eventually reach millions of consumers.
paragraphs.D: The job combines scientific rigour with a genuine passion for food and the experience it creates.
paragraphs.E: Professional tasting is, in this sense, as much an intellectual discipline as a physical one.
paragraphs.F: They must also be able to compare each sample objectively against a benchmark they hold only in memory.
paragraphs.G: Physical wellbeing and sensory performance are therefore more closely linked in this profession than in almost any other.

questions.explanation: E describe el catado profesional como tanto una disciplina intelectual como física, apoyando la introducción sobre las exigencias del trabajo más allá del placer.
F menciona la necesidad de comparar muestras objetivamente con una referencia guardada solo en la memoria, apoyando la atención al detalle y la memoria sensorial entrenada.
C señala que son los primeros fuera del laboratorio en probar productos que llegarán a millones de consumidores, contextualizando el rol de los catadores de desarrollo de producto.
A indica que desarrollar este vocabulario es uno de los aspectos más laboriosos del entrenamiento, conectando con la descripción del extenso vocabulario técnico.
G afirma que el bienestar físico y el rendimiento sensorial están más ligados en esta profesión que en casi cualquier otra, apoyando los efectos sobre el apetito y la fatiga del paladar.
D describe el trabajo como una combinación de rigor científico y pasión genuina, cerrando el artículo con la satisfacción de los catadores profesionales.
